query: Rafah crossing reopening February 2026 daily passenger limits medical evacuees

{"query":"Rafah crossing reopening February 2026 daily passenger limits medical evacuees","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1125,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://news.un.org/en/story/2026/02/1166872","title":"Gaza: Limited Rafah crossing reopening sparks hope – but also ‘massive trepidation’ | UN News","content":"Reopening the Rafah crossing was an integral part of the 20-point peace plan put forward by US President Donald Trump last September, with the ceasefire announced days later.
OCHA welcomed the reopening of the key border crossing, underscoring that “civilians must be allowed to leave and return voluntarily and safely, as international law requires.”
In a protest reflecting the extent of their despair, dozens of injured Palestinians staged a demonstration near the hospital, sitting in their wheelchairs and demanding an increase in the number of people allowed to travel daily which “should be in the hundreds” instead of 50.
“In this building alone, there are about 100 patients in need of medical referrals, and the number of wounded waiting to travel reaches about 13,000 patients and injured people.
The last medical evacuation through the Rafah crossing was in May 2024.
Overall, more than 18,500 patients in Gaza, including 4,000 children, are still waiting to access treatment abroad."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/1/israel-partially-reopens-rafah-crossing-in-pilot","title":"Israel says Gaza’s Rafah crossing to reopen for limited travel on Monday | News | Al Jazeera","content":"In the first few days, approximately 200 people, patients and their family members, will transit ‌daily into Egypt through the crossing, the officials said, with 50 people returning to Gaza per day.
Reopening ‌the border crossing was a key requirement of the first phase of United States President Donald Trump’s ⁠”ceasefire” to end Israel’s genocidal war."},{"url":"https://abc30.com/post/medical-evacuees-gaza-enter-egypt-rafah-crossing-reopens/18529058/","title":"Medical evacuees from Gaza enter Egypt as the Rafah crossing reopens - ABC30 Fresno","content":"About 20,000 Palestinian children and adults needing medical care hope to leave the devastated territory via the crossing, according to Gaza health officials.
About 150 hospitals across Egypt are ready to receive Palestinian patients evacuated from Gaza through Rafah, authorities said.
The crossing was briefly opened for the evacuation of medical patients during a ceasefire in early 2025."},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/live/2026/feb/02/rafah-border-egypt-gaza-israel-opens-crossing-latest-news-updates","title":"First medical evacuee leaves Gaza as Rafah crossing reopens for handful of Palestinians – as it happened | Gaza | The Guardian","content":"The first medical evacuee from Gaza has entered Egypt after the Rafah crossing reopened on Monday.
About 150 people were due to leave the territory on Monday, and 50 to enter it, according to Egyptian officials, more than 20 months after Israeli forces closed the crossing.
The head of a Palestinian technocratic committee established to oversee the day-to-day governance of Gaza said Monday that Israel’s reopening of the Rafah border crossing offered a “window of hope” for Palestinians in the territory.
Israel’s military struck several locations in south Lebanon on Monday, including in two villages where it issued evacuation warnings, saying it was hitting Hezbollah targets, AFP reported."},{"url":"https://www.lemonde.fr/en/international/article/2026/02/02/gaza-s-rafah-crossing-reopened-under-restrictive-israeli-conditions_6750045_4.html","title":"Gaza's Rafah crossing reopened under restrictive Israeli conditions","content":"Sick and wounded Gazans began crossing into Egypt to seek medical treatment on Monday, February 2, after Israel permitted a limited reopening of the Palestinian territory's Rafah border post.
Egyptian state-linked media reported that 50 people were expected to cross in each direction in the first days of the reopening.
The director of Gaza City's Al-Shifa Hospital, Mohammed Abu Salmiya, said there were 20,000 patients in the territory, including 4,500 children, in urgent need of treatment."},{"url":"https://reliefweb.int/report/occupied-palestinian-territory/news-quote-under-limited-opening-rafah-border-urgent-medical-evacuations-would-take-over-year","title":"Under the limited opening of Rafah border, urgent medical ...","content":"Feb 2, 2026 — The crossing will allow only 150 people to leave and 50 to return each day, despite 20,000 people, including 4,000 children, needing medical ..."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Rafah crossing reopening February 2026 daily passenger limits medical evacuees","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://media.un.org/unifeed/en/asset/d353/d3532438","title":"GAZA / RAFAH CROSSING OPENING | UNifeed - UN Media","content":"Feb 2, 2026 — The Rafah border crossing partially reopened after more than a year and a half of closure, allowing a limited number of patients to leave","score":0.8452414,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.latimes.com/world-nation/story/2026-02-01/gazas-rafah-crossing-prepares-for-limited-travel-to-resume","title":"Gaza's Rafah crossing prepares for limited travel to resume","content":"Feb 1, 2026 — Initial capacity is severely limited: Only 50 patients plus relatives can depart daily, and 50 Palestinians outside can return.","score":0.84277284,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://abc7ny.com/post/medical-evacuees-gaza-enter-egypt-rafah-crossing-reopens/18529058","title":"Medical evacuees from Gaza enter Egypt as the Rafah ...","content":"Feb 2, 2026 — Medical evacuees from Gaza entered Egypt on Monday as the Rafah border crossing reopened, marking a key step in the Israel-Hamas ceasefire.","score":0.80405265,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/feb/02/israel-agrees-to-limited-reopening-of-rafah-border-crossing-in-gaza","title":"Handful of sick and wounded Palestinians allowed through Rafah crossing on first day | Gaza | The Guardian","content":"Palestinians hope for more after limited Rafah crossing opening – video. A small number of sick and wounded Palestinians have begun crossing into Egypt to seek medical treatment after Israel permitted a limited reopening of the Palestinian territory’s Rafah border post as fragile diplomatic efforts to stabilise the conflict inch forward. About 150 people were due to leave the territory on Monday, and 50 to enter it, according to Egyptian officials, more than 20 months after Israeli forces closed the crossing. However, by nightfall, Reuters reported that Israel had permitted 12 Palestinians to re-enter the territory, according to Palestinian and Egyptian sources. The crossing had been closed since Israeli troops seized it in May 2024, only briefly opening during a ceasefire in early 2025 for the evacuation of medical patients. Israel has made clear that all movement through the crossing after the partial reopening will be subject to joint Israeli-Egyptian security screening and that, for now, only a small number of Gaza’s tens of thousands of wounded and ill Palestinians will be permitted to leave each day.","score":0.8021998,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-02-04/desperate-need-for-medical-evacuations-in-gaza/106298622","title":"Desperate need for medical evacuations in Gaza as Rafah ...","content":"Feb 3, 2026 — Gaza's key Rafah border crossing with Egypt has been reopened. However, Israeli authorities have allowed fewer than 30 Palestinians to enter or ...Read more","score":0.7797419,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.61,"request_id":"ff35f7a1-14e3-4007-986b-c7ee1bfae1e8"}
